rulings.Case Title: EDWARD SIRIAS VS. THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA et al

rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 13, 2015, Line 1, DEFENDANT THE REGENTS OF THE UNIVERSITY OF CALIFORNIA'S Motion To Set Deposition Fee. Pro Tem Judge Nils Rosenquest, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Continued to August 27, 2015, to give moving party the opportunity to comply with Local Rule 2.6B. Moving party must deliver courtesy copies of the opposition papers to Department 302, with a cover letter stating the new hearing date, no later than August 13, 2015 at 4:00 pm or the motion will be denied. Any exhibit must be separated by an exhibit tab per CRC 3.1110(f). Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: PATRICIA REBOLLEDO et al VS. UNION BANK, NA et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 13, 2015, Line 2, DEFENDANT UNITED SECURITY BANK'S MOTION FOR SUMMARY ADJUDICATION. Defendant's Motion for Summary Adjudication of the sixth cause of action is GRANTED. Defendant's evidence shifts the burden by establishing Braly was unaware that Alvarado was a United Bank employee, that United Bank prohibited loans between employees, or that the check was a loan between Rebolledo and Alvarado. Plaintiffs fail to present substantial, non-speculative evidence to dispute these facts; without such knowledge, United Securities Bank cannot be liable for this cause of action because there is no proof of knowledge or an intentional attempt to interfere with Plaintiffs' employment contracts with United Bank. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and in compliance with Code of Civil Procedure 437c(g) and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: TIMOTHY CAUWELS et al VS. DANA JOHNSON et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Summary Judgment
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 13, 2015, Line 3, PLAINTIFF TIMOTHY CAUWELS, LESLIE CAUWELS' Motion For Reconsideration Of Order Granting Summary Judgment. Defendant's Notice Of Motion And Motion To Recall And Or Quash Writ Of Execution And Vacate Execution Of Levy And Liens is granted. Judgment entered by this court on July 20, 2011, provides plaintiffs with their monetary damages through a constructive trust. It is clear from the language of the Judgment that the court is limiting the source of the recovery to the post-judgment royalties. The Court lists the gross amount of the judgment as $1,238,379.00, then states that the Defendants are entitled to a credit of $882,267.00, then states that the net of Plaintiff's judgment after accounting for the defendants' credit is $460,391 in addition to $72,342.00 in pre-judgment interest. Defendant's Request for Judicial Notice is granted. Plaintiff's Request for Judicial Notice is granted. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: MUSEUM PLAZA, LLC A CALIFORNIA LIMITED LIABILITY VS. WEST BUILDERS, INC. A CALIFORNIA CORPORATION et al
rulings.Calendar Matter: Notice Of Motion And Motion To Lift Arbitration Stay Or, Alternatively, To Compel West Builders, Inc. To Participate In Arbitration Proceedings And For Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant West Builders, Inc. And Its Counsel Of Record Pursuant To Ccp 128.5
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 13, 2015, Line 4, PLAINTIFF MUSEUM PLAZA, LLC'S Motion To Lift Arbitration Stay Or, Alternatively, To Compel West Builders, Inc. To Participate In Arbitration Proceedings And For Imposition Of Monetary Sanctions Against Defendant West Builders, Inc. And Its Counsel Of Record Pursuant To Ccp 128.5. DENIED. Once a matter is referred to arbitration, the Court retains only "vestigial" jurisdiction over the matter, limited to "making the determination, upon conclusion of the arbitration proceedings, of whether there was an award on the merits (in which case the action at law should be dismissed because of the res judicata effects of the arbitration award) or not (at which point the action at law may resume to determine the rights of the parties). (Brock v. Kaiser Foundation Hospitals (1992) 10 Cal.App.4th 1790, 1796.) If Museum Plaza does not wish to advance West's fees in order to preserve the arbitration, its remedy is to wait for the arbitrator to dismiss the matter based on West's non-payment of fees, then move to have the matter returned to the trial calendar on the basis that such a dismissal is not an "award" under California Code of Civil Procedure section 1281.4. (See Cinel v. Cristopher (2012) 203 Cal.App.4th 759.) Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)

rulings.Case Title: JORGE VALERA VS. FORD MOTOR COMPANY ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: Plaintiff Jorge Valera'S Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff'S Request For Production Of Documents, Set One
rulings.Rulings: Matter on calendar for Thursday, August 13, 2015, Line 6, Plaintiff Jorge Valera's Motion To Compel Further Responses To Plaintiff's Request For Production Of Documents, Set One. Pro Tem Judge Steven Stein, a member of the California State Bar who meets all the requirements set forth in CRC 2.812 to serve as a temporary judge, has been assigned to hear this motion. Prior to the hearing all parties to the motion will be asked to sign a stipulation agreeing that the motion may be heard by the Pro Tem Judge. If all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the hearing will proceed before the Judge Pro Tem who will decide the motion with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. If a party appears by telephone, the stipulation may be signed via fax or consent to sign given by email. If not all parties to the motion sign the stipulation, the Pro Tem Judge will hold a hearing on the motion and, based on the papers submitted by the parties and the hearing, issue a report in the nature of a recommendation to the Dept. 302 Judge, who will then decide the motion. If a party does not appear at the hearing, the party will be deemed to have stipulated that the motion will be decided by the Pro Tem Judge with the same authority as a Superior Court Judge. The Pro Tem Judge has issued the following tentative ruling: Hearing required. The motion to compel is granted. The Court agrees with the defendant document request categories which seek "any and all" documents in a Song-Beverly Consumer Warranty Act action is overbroad and potentially covers millions of documents. The Court intends to modify, when needed, each specific document request category to focus on allegations of the subject vehicle's "Electric Battery Defect". The Court finds Donlen v. Ford Motor Company, 217 Cal. App. 4th 138 (2013) and Doppes v. Bentley Motors, Inc., 174 Cal. App 4th 967 (2009) support the broad scope of discoverable documentary evidence requested. The parties are directed to "meet and confer" prior to the hearing to attempt to reach agreement on reformulated and focused document request language. The Court agrees with defendant a Protective Order/Confidentiality Order is appropriate for many of the documents to be produced. The Court will hear argument as to the necessity of a "highly confidential" document designation in the context of the evolving/experimental technology of electric cars. All documents to which attorney client privilege is claimed are to be identified in a privilege log. Plaintiff's request for "prospective monetary damages" is denied. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to sbs@knoxricksen.com with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. If the tentative ruling is not contested, the parties are deemed to have stipulated to the Pro Tem hearing the motion and the Pro Tem will sign an order confirming the tentative ruling. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order repeating verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/JPT)

rulings.Case Title: SARAH PERLISS VS. SCOTT POTTER ET AL
rulings.Calendar Matter: DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT
rulings.Rulings: Set for hearing on Thursday, August 13, 2015, Line 11, DEFENDANT SCOTT POTTER, SKP CAPITAL PARTNERS' DEMURRER TO COMPLAINT. Sustained with ten days leave to amend. No opposition filed. Any party who contests a tentative ruling must send an email to contestdept302tr@sftc.org with a copy to all other parties by 4pm stating, without argument, the portion(s) of the tentative ruling that the party contests. The subject line of the email shall include the line number, case name and case number. The prevailing party is required to prepare a proposed order with the name and address of the prevailing party's counsel or the prevailing party if pro per in the top left of the first page of proposed order which repeats verbatim the substantive portion of the tentative ruling and must bring the proposed order to the hearing or email it to contestdept302tr@sftc.org prior to the hearing even if the motion is not opposed or the tentative ruling is not contested. =(302/EG)
